_comment: EVERY global tunable lives here (design pillar #2). Game hot-reloads this file in dev. Values documented in WEB_GAME_ROADMAP.md are marked; the rest are PLACEHOLDER pending CLAUDE.md verification.

skills._source: ROADMAP_NEXT B5-1: positive skill gains are multiplied by (1 - level/max)^growthCurveExp; 0 restores linear gains, higher values slow growth near max.

autonomy._source: seek/stop from roadmap §2; cooldown PLACEHOLDER — verify vs CLAUDE.md. decisionGraceSeconds (B13-7): sim-time grace window suppressing AUTONOMOUS decisions right after a 'fresh start' moment (boot, map switch, return from work, return from a visit) so the Sim doesn't act before the player gets their bearings; default 5 sim-seconds, PLACEHOLDER pending designer tuning. Player-issued orders are never affected.

time._source: 60 s/day is the Unreal DEBUG speed — open decision #2, retune in Phase 1

ambience._source: B13-9/B13-10 shared room-aware ambience. radiusMeters is the maximum sim-to-device range for both night light comfort and sleep blockers. Walls and closed doors block; open apertures connect. nightComfortEnabled and sleepBlockingEnabled are independent master switches; comfortNeedId names the need receiving each light's sparse light.comfortBonus per needs tick.

textures._source: ROADMAP_NEXT B9-1 (floor/wall image textures). metersPerTile = how many meters of surface one texture repeat spans, so a tiled floor/wall reads at a physical scale regardless of the room size. Per-surface texture image paths live on data/maps/<map>.floors[].texture / walls[].texture. See game/textures.ts (repeat math) + world.ts (loader/UV wiring).

map._source: which data/maps/<active>.json the game plays; set from the Map Editor "Play this map" button

buy._source: ROADMAP_NEXT B13-8: opacity of the actual asset mesh/sprite shown beside the green/red footprint while placing or moving in Buy Mode. Clamped to 0..1; game default is 0.5 when absent.

credit._source: PROJECT_CONTEXT.md §7.24 F3. Score changes are signed deltas. Debt windows scale linearly from the low-score factor at min to the high-score factor at max.

quests._source: Quest system slice (PROJECT_CONTEXT.md §3) — toast on-screen time and how many completed quests the HUD log keeps.

visa._source: Visa system V1 (PROJECT_CONTEXT.md §7.20 B3-6) — must match an id in data/visas.json; game/visas.ts's VisaMachine falls back to "visitor" if this whole block is absent.

phone._source: Smartphone/jobs V2 + designer HUD launcher delta (PROJECT_CONTEXT.md §7.20) — jobListSize controls hourly listings; icon is the persistent HUD button image. rentalTabName (ROADMAP_APT R1, §6.4 RESOLVED) and contactsTabName (ROADMAP_SOCIAL S5) are renameable phone-tab labels consumed by the HUD.

work._source: Going to work V3 + B6-5 (§7.20/§7.28). autoSpeed applies while away. Promotion chance = level promoteChancePercent × happiness/100 × promotionHappinessFactor, clamped to 0..100. B7-5: departureWindowHours = hours after startHour the sim may LEAVE for work (clamped to the shift duration); past it the shift is MISSED (skip registers at window close). B7-6 (deterministic auto-departure): while the window is open the sim leaves on its own iff happiness >= autoDepartHappinessMin AND energy >= autoDepartEnergyMin; below either it stays home.

bills._source: Bills system (PROJECT_CONTEXT.md §7.22) — in-game days between recurring arrivals from data/bills.json.

interaction._source: Asset facing slice (PROJECT_CONTEXT.md §7.2). useSpotClearance = gap beyond an asset's footprint edge for a front-approach stand point; seatViewDistance = how far in front of a seat-aware target (e.g. the TV) the viewing point sits when ranking candidate seats — ports the Unreal prototype's RightVector*400 (~4m) at a web-appropriate scale. seatSearchRadius (ROADMAP_NEXT item 2, 2026-07-15) = max distance from a seat-aware target's own position a candidate seat may be to count as 'eligible' — beyond it, findSeatFor returns null and the sim sits on the ground instead (game/sim.ts's groundSit).

fire._source: ROADMAP_NEXT item 6 (fire destruction + spreading, PROJECT_CONTEXT.md §7.3 follow-on). burnSeconds = how long an unextinguished 'fire' instance burns before destroying its base object (replaced by a 'pile of ash' transient — see assets.json's ash row); spreadRadius is meters, independent from map gridSize, and controls how far a live fire scans each tick for AssetDef.combustibility-bearing neighbors to roll spread onto. Sim-time seconds, same clock as needs decay/gains/doors. panicSeconds (ROADMAP_NEXT B2-5) = how long the sim plays the 'panic' animation state whenever ANY fire spawns (initial risk roll or spread) before autonomy resumes — see game/accidents.ts's onFireSpawned hook + game/main.ts's triggerPanic.

fillBar._source: Designer request (2026-07-16): a small in-world fill indicator over each placed garbage can, mirroring game/progressbar.ts's camera-space sprite bar. widthMeters/heightMeters/yOffsetMeters size and position it above the can's own placed position (yOffsetMeters, not tied to a character height); fillColor/trackColor are CSS hex strings (THREE.SpriteMaterial accepts either a numeric or string hex); showWhenEmpty=false hides the bar entirely at fill 0. hideWhenOccluded=true (2026-07-17) hides a can's bar while the can is behind a wall/another asset from the current camera (cheap throttled raycast, game/garbage.ts's GarbageFillBarController.updateOcclusion) — only these garbage fill bars are occlusion-tested; the sim's action/skill bars stay always-visible. All fields optional/sparse — see game/garbage.ts's DEFAULT_GARBAGE_FILLBAR fallback and GarbageFillBarController.

garbage._source: ROADMAP_NEXT item 10 (garbage/tidying). autoTidyRadius (meters) = how close a non-full garbage can must be for the sim to deposit waste itself instead of dropping a transient; cleanlinessThreshold = the minimum value of the personality stat named by cleanlinessVar (stats.json's personality[] family) required to bother; see game/garbage.ts's decideWasteHandling.

waste._source: ROADMAP_NEXT B6-4. Every waste-producing completion creates one baseline item; this is the chance of one extra item, linearly interpolated from extraAtMin at stat 0 to extraAtMax at that stat's authored maximum.

doors._source: Doors-as-assets slice (PROJECT_CONTEXT.md §7.1). Defaults for AssetDef.door fields a door instance doesn't override. 0.5s open/close is the designer's requested default; triggerDistance is meters, independent from map gridSize, sized so the door swings open a beat before the sim (walkSpeed 2 m/s) reaches it.

view._source: ROADMAP_NEXT B6-9. Height in meters used by the player's in-page wall-cut toggle; view-only and never serialized. wallTopColor (B9-1 follow-up): flat unlit color for every wall's top face, giving the wall-cut view an architecture-plan look regardless of per-wall textures.

facade._source: ROADMAP_APT D3. Spacing in meters between the vertical mullion posts drawn across a curtain-wall (walls[].kind === 'curtainWall') glazed facade. See game/wallaperture.ts resolveMullionSpacing (falls back to 1.2m when absent/non-positive).

marker._source: Overhead marker slice (PROJECT_CONTEXT.md §7.7, Sims plumbob reference). Edit through tools/animations.html's Overhead marker section. Blank mesh = the built-in green octahedron; point it at a .glb or an image (png/jpg/webp/gif billboard) to override. Tuned against heightMeters 1.55: yOffset clears a real head, scale keeps a modest ~0.3m marker, spin/bob read as a gentle idle.

character._source: edit this block through tools/animations.html (Animation Mapper): it lists the GLB's actual clips, previews them in 3D, and maps them to states. animationPaths = extra GLBs whose clips are merged in (Mixamo one-clip-per-file exports, same skeleton). sitHeight/lieHeight replace the old hardcoded 0.25/0.55. Missing/failed GLB keeps the capsule stand-in.

audio._source: ROADMAP_NEXT item 7 (audio placeholders). masterVolume/musicVolume/sfxVolume are 0..1 and multiply together per channel; musicCrossfadeSeconds is how long a music CONTEXT switch (map <-> buy mode) takes; buyModeMusic is the fixed track played while in Buy/Sell mode. See game/audio.ts's resolveAudioTuning for defaults when this whole block is absent.

bladderFailure._source: ROADMAP_NEXT B2-4 (bladder failure). durationSeconds = how long the sim plays the 'pee' animation before autonomy resumes; reliefAmount (0..100) = bladder's value right after the accident (a minimal top-up, not a full refill — the sim likely still wants a real bathroom trip). ROADMAP_NEXT B3-2: hygieneAfter (0..100) = hygiene's value right after the accident too (an absolute set, like reliefAmount — not a delta); 0 = fully soiled. game/bladder.ts's checkBladderFailure re-arms as soon as the failure event COMPLETES (main.ts calls initBladderFailureState's re-arm at that point, ROADMAP_NEXT B3-3) rather than waiting on bladder to climb back above reliefAmount via decay alone, which can never happen without a real bathroom trip.

energyCollapse._source: ROADMAP_NEXT B6-14. At zero energy: collapse, lie_sleep on the ground for sleepSeconds, then refill energyAfter and re-arm. All durations are simulation seconds.

starvation.message: Your Sim starved after going too long without food.
starvation._source: ROADMAP_NEXT B6-15. Hunger at zero starts the countdown; eating above recoveryThreshold cancels it. Expiry plays starve before terminal game over.

feedback._source: ROADMAP_NEXT B6-16. Projected HTML feedback above the sim; duration is simulation seconds, rise is screen pixels, yOffset is meters above character height. skillBar (2026-07-17): the second world bar shown near the sim whenever the current action grants a skill, tracking the primary (largest-gain) skill's progress toward its next point; stacked gapMeters above the action progress bar with a distinct fillColor. All fields optional/sparse — see game/progressbar.ts's SKILL_BAR_DEFAULTS/createSkillBarInstance.

food._source: ROADMAP_NEXT B7-2: a COOKED meal's hunger fulfillment scales with cooking skill. effectiveHungerGain = base hungerGain (data/assets.json 'meal' = 45) * lerp(cookHungerAtSkill0, cookHungerAtSkillMax, skills.cooking/max). Novice (cooking 0) fills 60% (27), master (cooking at max 100) fills 150% (67.5, clamped by need cap). Snacks (fridge) unaffected.

camera._source: PLACEHOLDER — clamps to be tuned against Sims reference screenshots. rotateSpeedDegPerPx = desktop right-drag yaw sensitivity; twistDeadzoneDeg/twistSpeed = mobile two-finger twist (coexists with pinch-zoom), deadzone keeps pinch jitter from spinning the camera
